rationale.R1.1: Section makes explicit contribution claims ('eliminates self-preference bias', 'across model families and evaluation regimes') that require reconstruction and validity assessment.
rationale.R1.2: Conclusion ('eliminates' bias) does not strictly follow from the data (gap 0.6→0.05, pairwise 71%→52%), and the generalization to 'model families' is unsupported by single-judge experiments; logical consistency is materially at risk.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: section is dense with specific numerical claims (κ 0.31→0.68, 62% human preference, 52% post-calibration pairwise rate). Verifying that stated numbers coherently support the stated conclusions is the primary technical obligation.
rationale.R1.6: Any 'review' of a written section carries baseline expectation of sentence- and paragraph-level writing quality assessment, making this centrally expected.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize scientific/technical scrutiny — checking whether the empirical numbers logically support the claims (making R1.2 and R1.3 load-bearing at 1.0, R1.6 secondary) — or editorial polish — improving writing clarity and flow (making R1.6 load-bearing at 1.0, R1.2/R1.3 secondary)?
